reasoning: The likelihood of Tesla deploying a completely uncrewed, non-tester ride-hailing service in 2025 that achieves 10,000 public non-tester rides is historically aligned with a near-zero base rate, given Elon Musk's long history of delayed FSD promises. The strict criteria (no backup driver, no observers, non-tester rides, no teleoperation dependency, over 400m per trip) essentially demands a full L4 Waymo-equivalent public commercial service launched and scaled within single year. Waymo and Cruise took years of pilot programs before achieving such scale. Even if Tesla releases unsupervised FSD in Texas in late 2025, operating a commercially viable 'Uber-style' network producing 10k valid trips by year's end is extremely improbable.
